content.text:
File: docs/INTENSITY_PRINCIPLE.md
Lines 1-76:
 1 | # Intensity Beats Extensity — Every Time
 2 | 
 3 | > **The core marketing doctrine for all projects.**
 4 | 
 5 | ---
 6 | 
 7 | ## The Principle
 8 | 
 9 | **One channel, mastered deeply, will outperform five channels covered shallowly. Every. Single. Time.**
10 | 
11 | This isn't a preference. It's a law of focus. Marketing success comes from:
12 | 
13 | 1. **Depth of message** — One offer, refined to razor sharpness, hitting the exact nerve of the exact persona
14 | 2. **Depth of channel** — One platform understood at the algorithm level, not the surface level
15 | 3. **Depth of frequency** — Showing up consistently in one place until you're unavoidable, not flickering across many
16 | 4. **Depth of creative** — One piece of content so good it travels on its own, not ten pieces that need pushing
17 | 
18 | ## What This Means in Practice
19 | 
20 | ### ❌ Extensity (what we don't do)
21 | - Launching on 7 social platforms simultaneously
22 | - Running Google Ads + Meta Ads + TikTok + LinkedIn Ads all at once with thin budgets
23 | - Writing blog posts for SEO, social posts for engagement, email sequences for nurture, and a podcast for authority — all half-baked
24 | - Spreading $5K/month across 10 channels ($500 each, none getting traction)
25 | - Copy-pasting the same content across 6 platforms with minor tweaks
26 | 
27 | ### ✅ Intensity (what we do)
28 | - **Pick ONE channel** that matches where the persona actually lives
29 | - **Pour the full budget** into that one channel until it compounds
30 | - **Create ONE hero piece** of content per week so good it would be embarrassing to competitor
31 | - **Run ONE ad campaign** with enough daily spend to get statistical significance in days, not months
32 | - **Build ONE funnel** and optimize every step until conversion is best-in-class
33 | - **Dominate one keyword cluster** before touching a second
34 | 
35 | ## The Intensity Checklist
36 | 
37 | Before approving any marketing project, ask:
38 | 
39 | - [ ] **Is this focused on ONE primary channel?** If more than two, cut.
40 | - [ ] **Is the budget concentrated enough to generate real signal?** Thin spreads produce noise, not data.
41 | - [ ] **Is there ONE hero offer being pushed?** Multiple offers dilute attention and confuse tracking.
42 | - [ ] **Is the creative quality high enough to win on its own merit?** If it needs paid distribution to be seen, it's not intense enough.
43 | - [ ] **Are we going deep on one persona before expanding?** Message-market fit with one audience before adding a second.
44 | - [ ] **Is there a single primary KPI we're optimizing for?** Not 12 vanity metrics. One number that matters.
45 | 
46 | ## How This Applies to the Marketing Hub
47 | 
48 | The Marketing Hub has 30 sections. That's the toolkit. But **no single campaign should activate all 30 simultaneously.**
49 | 
50 | | Phase | Intensity Focus | What We Use |
51 | |---|---|---|
52 | | **Phase 1: Dominate** | Pick the highest-ROI channel + persona combo | Personas, Campaigns, Copy Generator, one channel in Social Studio |
53 | | **Phase 2: Convert** | Build the world's best funnel for that channel | Landing Pages, Forms, A/B Testing, CRM, Automations |
54 | | **Phase 3: Measure** | Know that one channel better than anyone | Analytics, Attribution, SEO Toolkit (for organic) or Ads Manager (for paid) |
55 | | **Phase 4: Expand** | ONLY after channel #1 is compounding, add channel #2 | Repeat Phase 1-3 for new channel |
56 | 
57 | ## The Anti-Pattern We Reject
58 | 
59 | > "We need to be everywhere our customers might be."
60 | 
61 | No. You need to be **unmissable** in the one place they definitely are. Being everywhere means being nowhere. Being nowhere means being forgotten. Being forgotten means being irrelevant.
62 | 
63 | ## The Rule of One
64 | 
65 | - **One** persona to start
66 | - **One** channel to dominate
67 | - **One** offer to push
68 | - **One** hero creative per cycle
69 | - **One** KPI to optimize
70 | - **One** funnel to perfect
71 | 
72 | Master the one. Then — and only then — add the second.
73 | 
74 | ---
75 | 
76 | *This principle governs all marketing projects, campaigns, and strategies. When in doubt, go deeper, not wider.*


---

File: docs/HEURISTICS_AUDIT.md
Lines 1-120:
  1 | # Heuristics & Similar-Knowledge Audit — Marketing Hub
  2 | 
  3 | > **Status: IMPLEMENTED.** All 16 entries below have been replaced with real
  4 | > models, calibrated scoring, or doctrine-labeled checklists. The shared
  5 | > logistic-regression module lives at `src/lib/ml/logistic.ts`. Each entry's
  6 | > "Replace" section now describes what was shipped, not what to do.
  7 | >
  8 | > **Goal: leave no heuristic undocumented.** Every hand-tuned rule, magic
  9 | > number, regex-threshold, fixed weight, and doctrinal formula in the hub is
 10 | > listed here with: the actual rule, the data it rests on, how to measure it,
 11 | > how to tell it apart from a real model, and the concrete replacement ready to
 12 | > implement.
 13 | 
 14 | ## Legend
 15 | 
 16 | - **HEURISTIC** — a rule with weights/thresholds chosen by a human, never
 17 |   re-fit, same for every tenant. Replace with a fitted model.
 18 | - **DOCTRINE** — a cited framework from a named master or peer-reviewed paper
 19 |   (Cialdini, Schwartz, Kennedy, Porter, Lynn, Rogers). Keep, but expose
 20 |   calibration so users can see how it performs on their data.
 21 | - **FAKE-DATA** — code that synthesizes numbers instead of measuring them.
 22 |   Flag and remove or replace with real instrumentation.
 23 | 
 24 | ## Operating rule (applies to every entry below)
 25 | 
 26 | A heuristic must ship with its **own measurement**: calibration curve, AUC,
 27 | Cohen's kappa, or statistical power — computed from real hub data and shown in
 28 | the UI next to the score. A heuristic you can't measure is a guess. A
 29 | heuristic you can measure either earns its place (beats base rate) or gets
 30 | replaced.
 31 | 
 32 | The replacement order, by ROI:
 33 | 1. `experiments.ts` — one-line fix, biggest credibility win.
 34 | 2. `attribution.ts` Markov removal-effect — uses path data already stored.
 35 | 3. `icp.ts` + `intent.ts` + `deal-intel.ts` — one shared logistic-regression
 36 |    module, three consumers.
 37 | 4. `quality-gate.ts` LLM-as-judge swap — needs a 200-sample human-graded
 38 |    calibration set first.
 39 | 5. `hub-intelligence.ts` + `patrick.ts` + `wire.ts` + `seo.ts` — score
 40 |    calibration against historical outcomes.
 41 | 6. `machine.ts` + `ideas/graduate` + `sabri.ts` — LLM-structured replacements.
 42 | 7. `analytics.ts` — remove synthetic data or label it as simulated.
 43 | 
 44 | ---
 45 | 
 46 | ## 1. ICP lead scoring — `src/lib/icp.ts:130-187`
 47 | 
 48 | **Rule.** `score = 0.3` base, then `+ industry.weight × 0.4`, `+0.1` if
 49 | industry win-rate > 0.5, `+0.15` if avg deal ≥ 80% of ICP avg (else `−0.1`
 50 | if < 30%), `+0.1` for senior-title regex, `+0.05` for having a company.
 51 | Clamp 0–1, grade A/B/C/D.
 52 | 
 53 | **Data.** `deals` (stage, value), `contacts` (industry, company, title). Real
 54 | data — but only 3 features used, weights invented, never fit.
 55 | 
 56 | **Measure.**
 57 | - Calibration curve: bucket scores into deciles, plot actual win rate per
 58 |   bucket vs the diagonal.

:1:function addIconImport(content: string, icon: string): string {
  if (iconImported(content, icon)) return content;
  return content.replace(
    /} from "lucide-react";/,
    `  ${icon},\n} from "lucide-react";`,
  );
}

function addToSidebar(content: string, spec: FeatureSpec): string {
  if (hrefInSidebar(content, spec.slug)) return content;
  const group = spec.group || "Channels";
  const groupIdx = content.indexOf(`group: "${group}"`);
  if (groupIdx === -1) {
    throw new Error(`Sidebar group "${group}" not found — add the nav entry manually`);
  }
  const closeIdx = content.indexOf("  ]},", groupIdx);
  if (closeIdx === -1) throw new Error(`Could not find group close for "${group}"`);
  const line = `    { href: "/${spec.slug}", label: ${JSON.stringify(spec.label)}, icon: ${spec.icon}, badge: "NEW" },\n`;
  let next = content.slice(0, closeIdx) + line + content.slice(closeIdx);
  next = addIconImport(next, spec.icon);
  return next;
}

function findItemsClose(source, fromIndex) {
  // source[fromIndex] should be '['
  let depth = 0;
  for (let i = fromIndex; i < source.length; i++) {
    const ch = source[i];
    if (ch === '[') depth++;
    else if (ch === ']') { depth--; if (depth === 0) return i; }
  }
  return -1;
}
```

Let me write it.

```ts
#!/usr/bin/env tsx
/**
 * add-feature.ts — Automate Marketing Hub feature addition.
 *
 * Scaffolds a new "seed surface" feature end-to-end:
 *   1. src/app/<slug>/page.tsx            (thin SeedSurface page)
 *   2. src/lib/seed-surfaces.ts           (registry entry)
 *   3. src/components/Sidebar.tsx          (nav entry + icon import)
 *
 * The generic /api/seed/[slug] route + seed-surface lib already handle
 * any slug, so no backend code is required.
 *
 * Usage:
 *   tsx scripts/add-feature.ts --slug newsletter --label Newsletter --icon Mail --group Channels --statuses draft,scheduled,sent --categories weekly,digest
 *   tsx scripts/add-feature.ts --batch scripts/features-batch.json
 */
import fs from "node:fs";
import path from "node:path";

const ROOT = process.cwd();
const APP_DIR = path.join(ROOT, "src/app");
const SEED_SURFACES = path.join(ROOT, "src/lib/seed-surfaces.ts");
const SIDEBAR = path.join(ROOT, "src/components/Sidebar.tsx");

type Spec = {
  slug: string;
  label: string;
  icon: string;
  group?: string;
  statuses?: string[];
  categories?: string[];
  seed?: Array<{ title: string; status: string; category: string; body?: string; url?: string; meta?: Record<string, unknown> }>;
};

function jsStr(s: string): string {
  return JSON.stringify(s);
}

function fail(msg: string): never {
  console.error("✖ " + msg);
  process.exit(1);
}

function read(p: string): string {
  return fs.readFileSync(p, "utf8");
}
function write(p: string, c: string): void {
  fs.writeFileSync(p, c, "utf8");
}

function slugExists(slug: string): boolean {
  const surfaces = read(SEED_SURFACES);
  return new RegExp(`slug:\\s*["']${slug}["']`).test(surfaces);
}

function pageExists(slug: string): boolean {
  return fs.existsSync(path.join(APP_DIR, slug, "page.tsx"));
}

// ---- page ----
function genPage(spec: Spec): string {
  return `"use client";
import { ${spec.icon} } from "lucide-react";
import { SeedSurface } from "@/components/seed-surface/SeedSurface";

export default function Page() {
  return <SeedSurface slug="${spec.slug}" label="${spec.label}" icon={<${spec.icon} className="w-7 h-7 text-indigo-500" />} />;
}
`;
}

// ---- seed-surfaces entry ----
function genEntry(spec: Spec): string {
  const statuses = spec.statuses && spec.statuses.length ? spec.statuses : ["draft", "active", "archived"];
  const categories = spec.categories && spec.categories.length ? spec.categories : ["general"];
  const seed = spec.seed && spec.seed.length ? spec.seed : [
    { title: `${spec.label} — Item 1`, status: statuses[0], category: categories[0], body: `Auto-generated ${spec.label.toLowerCase()} record.`, meta: {} },
    { title: `${spec.label} — Item 2`, status: statuses[1] || statuses[0], category: categories[0], body: "", meta: {} },
  ];
  const seedStr = seed.map((r) => {
    const meta = r.meta && Object.keys(r.meta).length ? JSON.stringify(r.meta) : "{}";
    return `      { title: ${jsStr(r.title)}, status: ${jsStr(r.status)}, category: ${jsStr(r.category)}, body: ${jsStr(r.body || "")}, meta: ${meta} }`;
  }).join(",\n");
  return `  {
    slug: ${jsStr(spec.slug)},
    label: ${jsStr(spec.label)},
    statuses: ${JSON.stringify(statuses)},
    categories: ${JSON.stringify(categories)},
    seed: [
${seedStr}
    ],
  },`;
}

function addToSeedSurfaces(spec: Spec): void {
  let src = read(SEED_SURFACES);
  const idx = src.lastIndexOf("];");
  if (idx === -1) fail("Could not find closing '];' in seed-surfaces.ts");
  src = src.slice(0, idx) + genEntry(spec) + "\n" + src.slice(idx);
  write(SEED_SURFACES, src);
}

// ---- sidebar ----
function ensureIconImport(icon: string): void {
  let src = read(SIDEBAR);
  if (new RegExp(`\\b${icon}\\b`).test(src.split(`} from "lucide-react";`)[0])) {
    return; // already imported
  }
  const marker = `} from "lucide-react";`;
  const i = src.indexOf(marker);
  if (i === -1) fail("Could not find lucide-react import in Sidebar.tsx");
  src = src.slice(0, i) + `  ${icon},\n` + src.slice(i);
  write(SIDEBAR, src);
}

function addToSidebar(spec: Spec): void {
  const group = spec.group || "Channels";
  let src = read(SIDEBAR);
  const entry = `    { href: "/${spec.slug}", label: ${jsStr(spec.label)}, icon: ${spec.icon}, badge: "NEW" },`;
  const groupIdx = src.indexOf(`group: ${jsStr(group)}`);
  if (groupIdx === -1) {
    // create a new group at end of NAV
    const navStart = src.indexOf("const NAV = [");
    if (navStart === -1) fail("Could not find NAV array in Sidebar.tsx");
    // find matching close of NAV
    let depth = 0, close = -1;
    for (let i = src.indexOf("[", navStart); i < src.length; i++) {
      if (src[i] === "[") depth++;
      else if (src[i] === "]") { depth--; if (depth === 0) { close = i; break; } }
    }
    if (close === -1) fail("Could not find NAV close");
    const newGroup = `  { group: ${jsStr(group)}, items: [\n${entry}\n  ]},\n`;
    src = src.slice(0, close) + newGroup + src.slice(close);
  } else {
    const close = src.indexOf("  ]},", groupIdx);
    if (close === -1) fail(`Could not find close of group "${group}"`);
    src = src.slice(0, close) + entry + "\n" + src.slice(close);
  }
  write(SIDEBAR, src);
}

// ---- orchestration ----
function addFeature(spec: Spec): void {
  if (!/^[a-z0-9]+(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*$/.test(spec.slug)) fail(`Invalid slug: ${spec.slug} (use lowercase kebab-case)`);
  if (!spec.label) fail("label is required");
  if (!spec.icon) fail("icon is required");
  if (slugExists(spec.slug)) fail(`Slug "${spec.slug}" already exists in seed-surfaces.ts`);
